tips_en: Start with GameCraft-Bench (Paper 2) for a sobering snapshot of where coding agents actually stand—best model at only 41.46% on playable end-to-end game generation. This frames why Paper 1's LoopCoder-v2 (a 21-point SWE-bench jump from a simple architectural choice: r=2 loops) and Paper 3's OPD-Evolver (closing in on much larger models via memory co-evolution) both matter. Read them as complementary efforts tackling the gap that GameCraft exposes: one sharpens the underlying code model, the other sharpens the agent's learning loop.
tips_zh: 建议先读 GameCraft-Bench（论文 2），它清晰呈现了当前编码代理的真实水平——最好的模型在端到端可玩游戏生成上仅得 41.46%，让人清醒认识差距所在。在此背景下，论文 1 的 LoopCoder-v2（仅凭一个简单的架构选择 r=2 循环就在 SWE-bench 上提升 21 分）和论文 3 的 OPD-Evolver（通过记忆协同进化逼近更大规模模型）都显得尤为关键。可将三者视为互补的攻关路线：GameCraft 揭示问题，LoopCoder 打磨底层代码模型，OPD-Evolver 则强化代理的学习闭环。
